finding: Exposure to goal-congruent content (prosocial game scenarios) increases subsequent target behavior (helping, cooperation) relative to neutral or opposing content, mediated by automatic activation of goal-congruent semantic knowledge accessible during performance.

Achievement trajectories diverge across language groups when initial status is examined, but converge when family SES is modeled as a mediator; school-level SES effects are weaker than family-level effects on growth parameters.

Autobiographical salience of stimuli is the strongest predictor of an emotion-memory blend (nostalgia); trait nostalgia proneness robustly predicts intensity independent of context, moderated by baseline personality dimensions and current mood state.

Cognitive demand on prior trials (N-1) improves performance on subsequent high-demand trials (N), independent of demand domain; effect is domain-specific for distractor interference but generalized across maintenance load.

Lower social class individuals allocate more resources to others in economic games and exhibit greater helping behavior; effect is mediated by higher endorsement of egalitarian values and experienced compassion.

Identical actions (unfair pricing, transgression) are judged as less acceptable and deserving harsher punishment when framed as future rather than past; effect is mediated by stronger anticipatory emotion for future events and eliminated under direct comparison.
